title: Sanger Tree of Life HMW DNA Extraction: Automated MagAttract v.3
author: Graeme Oatley
materials: For PowerMashed samples (weight less than 25 mg):

steps.title: Prepare a lysis buffer master mix
steps.instruction: Prepare a lysis buffer master mix: %s %s

steps.title: Transfer a 25 mg cryoprepped/bead beaten...
steps.instruction: Transfer a 25 mg cryoprepped/bead beaten sample into a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube, then hold on dry ice to keep the sample frozen.

steps.title: Add 374 µL of the lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 374 µL of the lysis buffer master mix to sample, then homogenise the sample and master mix by gently pipetting 10 times with a wide-bore pipette tip.

steps.title: Transfer the entire contents of the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the entire contents of the BioMasher tube to a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube using a wide-bore tip.

steps.title: Transfer sample into a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer sample into a 1.5 mL BioMasher II tube and add 374 µL lysis buffer.

steps.title: Disrupt sample in lysis buffer using...
steps.instruction: Disrupt sample in lysis buffer using the Diagnocine PowerMasher II tissue disruptor and BioMasher pestle, until no large pieces remain or sample cannot be disrupted further (for more detailed instructions regarding powermashing, please refer to the Sanger Tree of Life Sample Homogenisation: Powermash protocol).

steps.title: Centrifuge all sample tubes briefly and...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge all sample tubes briefly and incubate on the heat block at 25 ºC for 2 hours.

steps.title: While samples are lysing, label nine...
steps.instruction: While samples are lysing, label nine 1 mL 96-well deep-well KingFisher™ plates and fill the number of wells required for the number of samples in each plate as follows: %s %s

steps.title: Once samples have completed lysing, remove...
steps.instruction: Once samples have completed lysing, remove sample tubes from the heat block and briefly centrifuge to spin down.

steps.title: The Elution Plate is now the...
steps.instruction: The Elution Plate is now the ‘Sample Plate’ for the 0.45X SPRI.

steps.title: Inspect the Elution Plate for any...
steps.instruction: Inspect the Elution Plate for any magnetic beads in the wells. In the rare instance of magnetic beads remaining in the eluate (possible in viscous samples), these samples will need to be transferred to a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube and placed on a magnetic rack. Allow around 5 minutes for the beads to migrate and take the clear eluate containing the DNA using a wide-bore pipette tip. Return samples back to their original well following bead removal.

steps.title: Once the bead collection protocol has...
steps.instruction: Once the bead collection protocol has completed after approximately 5 minutes, follow the on-screen instructions to remove the plates from the instrument.

steps.title: The following morning, remove the plate...
steps.instruction: The following morning, remove the plate seal and return to the KingFisher™ Apex in order to run the ‘bead collection’ protocol – select this protocol from the protocol list and load the Tip Plate and Elution Plate as prompted by the instrument.

steps.title: Seal the Elution Plate with a...
steps.instruction: Seal the Elution Plate with a plate seal and leave overnight at room temperature to allow the DNA to elute.

steps.title: Once the protocol has completed, follow...
steps.instruction: Once the protocol has completed, follow the on-screen instructions to remove plates from the instrument. Make sure to retain the Tip Plate containing the tip comb for future steps.

steps.title: Once the final plate is loaded,...
steps.instruction: Once the final plate is loaded, the protocol will automatically begin; this takes approximately 40 minutes.

steps.title: Load the filled plates onto the...
steps.instruction: Load the filled plates onto the instrument following the instructions provided on screen.

steps.title: Select the required DNA extraction protocol...
steps.instruction: Select the required DNA extraction protocol in the protocol list on the KingFisher™ Apex (details in KingFisher™ Apex DNA Extraction Protocol section/attached file within the Materials section) and select using the play button.

steps.title: Using a wide-bore pipette tip, set...
steps.instruction: Using a wide-bore pipette tip, set the volume to 380 µL, transfer lysate from the sample tubes to individual wells in the sample plate, taking care not to transfer large pieces of debris if possible.

steps.title: Set-up the KingFisher™ plates for the 0
steps.instruction: Set-up the KingFisher™ plates for the 0.45X SPRI as detailed below: %s %s

steps.title: Store the DNA at 4 ºC
steps.instruction: Store the DNA at 4 ºC.

steps.title: Perform the required QC to determine...
steps.instruction: Perform the required QC to determine quantity and quality of the DNA extracted.

steps.title: Using a wide-bore pipette tip, transfer...
steps.instruction: Using a wide-bore pipette tip, transfer the 130 µL of eluate from the elution plate into microcentrifuge tubes.

steps.title: Once the protocol has completed, follow...
steps.instruction: Once the protocol has completed, follow the on-screen instructions to remove plates from the instrument.

steps.title: Once the final plate is loaded,...
steps.instruction: Once the final plate is loaded, the protocol will automatically begin; this will take approximately 50 minutes.

steps.title: Load the filled plates onto the...
steps.instruction: Load the filled plates onto the instrument following the instructions provided on screen.

steps.title: Select the required 0
steps.instruction: Select the required 0.45X SPRI protocol in the protocol list on the KingFisher™ Apex (details below in KingFisher™ Apex 0.45X SPRI Protocol section/attached file within the Materials section) and select using the play button.
